story: Data-center power-bill question: on-grid vs off-grid, and the cost-allocation gap

spine_check.explanation: Two independent sources (Northern Journal; Northern Alaska Environmental Center analysis of state lease docs) confirm the STAK North Slope campus is off-grid, behind-the-meter, disconnected from the Railbelt, so its fuel sales have no direct impact on city electricity prices.

verified_claims.statement: Alaska's DNR moved to transfer about 30 square miles of state land north of Houston in the Mat-Su to AIDEA for a multi-use industrial and energy development district that includes data centers.
verified_claims.value_detail: about 30 square miles (News Source 30; Beacon over 31); noncompetitive/no-cost transfer to AIDEA

evidence.verbatim_quote: 30 square miles of state land ... multi-use industrial and energy development district
Over 31 square miles of state-owned land north of Houston

verified_claims.notes: Use 'about 30 square miles' or 'more than 30 square miles'.

verified_claims.statement: AIDEA Executive Director Randy Ruaro said there are no pre-determined and set land use decisions made by AIDEA.

evidence.verbatim_quote: there are no pre-determined and set land use decisions made by AIDEA

verified_claims.statement: The DNR preliminary decision on the Mat-Su transfer runs 41 pages.

evidence.verbatim_quote: 41-page decision document

verified_claims.statement: Public comment on the Mat-Su land transfer is open through 5 p.m. on August 19, 2026, on the Alaska DNR website.

evidence.verbatim_quote: August 19 at 5 p.m. through the Alaska Department of Natural Resources website

verified_claims.statement: A Houston City Council member named Johansen introduced an ordinance in June to ban data centers within city limits, and the council voted to postpone the measure to its August 13 meeting.

evidence.verbatim_quote: An ordinance was introduced in June by Council Member Johansen to ban data centers within city limits ... voted Thursday to postpone a vote on the measure until members' next meeting Aug. 13

verified_claims.notes: Use 'Council Member Johansen' (first name not confirmed).

verified_claims.statement: Alaska currently has 8 data centers compared with Virginia's nation-leading 639.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Alaska currently has 8 data centers compared to Virginia's nation-leading 639

verified_claims.statement: STAK Energy's proposed North Slope AI and cloud campus sits about 25 miles south of Deadhorse just off the Dalton Highway and would self-generate a gigawatt or more of power, roughly 30 percent more than the peak demand of urban Alaska's entire grid.
verified_claims.value_detail: about 25 miles south of Deadhorse; a gigawatt or more (1 to 3 GW); ~30% more than urban grid peak

evidence.verbatim_quote: a gigawatt or more of power, which is some 30% more than the peak demand of urban Alaska's entire grid
1 to 3 gigawatts of on-site natural gas-fired generation ... about 26 miles south of Deadhorse and roughly one mile west of the Dalton Highway

verified_claims.statement: STAK Energy is seeking a 50-year lease of approximately 715.4 acres of state land for the campus.

evidence.verbatim_quote: approximately 715.4 acres of state land ... 50-year negotiated lease of state land

verified_claims.statement: The STAK campus would consume roughly 350 to 500 million standard cubic feet of North Slope gas per day.

evidence.verbatim_quote: roughly 350 to 500 million standard cubic feet of gas per day ... approximately 128 to 183 billion cubic feet per year

verified_claims.statement: The STAK public comment window closed July 17, 2026, having drawn more than 500 comments with fewer than a dozen in support, and STAK's John Boyle said some comments were form letters lacking substance.
verified_claims.value_detail: closed July 17, 2026; 500-plus comments; fewer than a dozen supportive

evidence.verbatim_quote: More than 500 public comments ... Fewer than a dozen comments endorsed the project ... Some of the comments are form letters lacking substance

verified_claims.attribution: John Boyle (STAK) for the 'form letters' quote

verified_claims.statement: Because the STAK North Slope project is off-grid and self-generating hundreds of miles from urban Alaska, reporting states its fuel sales would have no direct impact on city dwellers' electricity prices.
verified_claims.value_detail: off-grid; behind-the-meter; disconnected from the state power grid

evidence.verbatim_quote: sales of fuel to Stak would have no direct impact on city-dwellers' electricity prices ... The North Slope oil fields are hundreds of miles from urban Alaska and disconnected from the state's power grid
electricity consumed behind the meter by the computing facility rather than delivered to the Railbelt or another Alaska utility grid

verified_claims.statement: In an April 2026 ADN opinion piece, Launch Alaska's Penny Gage wrote that Chugach-area electric sales have declined about 1 percent a year for roughly 15 years, dropped another 2.3 percent in 2025, and that every 1 percent reduction leaves about 2.5 million dollars in fixed costs with nowhere to go, arguing a large new anchor load could spread those costs and lower rates.
verified_claims.value_detail: ~1%/yr for ~15 years; 2.3% in 2025; ~$2.5M fixed cost per 1% drop

evidence.verbatim_quote: declined for roughly 15 years, at about 1% annually ... electric sales dropped another 2.3% ... every 1% reduction in electric sales leaves approximately $2.5 million in fixed costs with nowhere to go

verified_claims.attribution: Penny Gage, Launch Alaska (ADN op-ed, April 2026)

verified_claims.statement: The Railbelt already faces a shortage of locally produced gas, which is why adding large new load raises a gas-supply concern rather than a wires concern.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Alaska's Railbelt already faces a shortage of locally produced gas

verified_claims.statement: A state bill by Rep. Donna Mears introduced in January 2026, HB 259, would set utility rate-setting guidelines and community-benefit standards for large data centers, but it was referred to the House Energy Committee and has not advanced.
verified_claims.value_detail: HB 259 (Rep. Donna Mears, Jan 2026); in House Energy Committee

evidence.verbatim_quote: House Bill 259, introduced by Rep. Donna Mears in January, which would create utility rate-setting guidelines and community engagement standards
HB 259 status (H) ENE (House Energy Committee) as of January 20, 2026 (per akleg BASIS bill detail; direct fetch 403, read via search index)

verified_claims.notes: Replaces dropped SB 250 claim. State-level rate-setting bill stuck in committee; the honest policy gap. akleg page 403s to direct fetch.

verified_claims.statement: In March 2026 the Anchorage Assembly adopted ordinance AO 2026-27 in a 10 to 2 vote, confining data centers to commercial and industrial zones, requiring landscaping buffers and enclosed power equipment, and requiring written statements from electric and water utilities that the grid has sufficient system capacity.
verified_claims.value_detail: AO 2026-27 adopted 10-2 (March 2026); Myers and McCormick against; sponsors Brawley and Volland

evidence.verbatim_quote: The Anchorage Assembly passed ordinance AO 2026-27 in a 10-2 vote ... only allowed in a commercial and industrial zones, not residential areas ... requires landscaping buffers and enclosed power equipment ... the grid has 'sufficient system capacity'

verified_claims.notes: ENACTED (10-2), not a proposal. Members Myers and McCormick voted against; sponsored by Anna Brawley and Daniel Volland. Background (March 2026).

verified_claims.statement: On July 16, 2026, Governor Dunleavy called a third Alaska LNG special session, to convene July 27, after the House deadlocked 19 to 19 and the Senate passed a gas-tax bill 11 to 8 that included a 9.4 percent pass-through tax he rejected.
verified_claims.value_detail: session called July 16, convenes July 27; House 19-19; Senate 11-8; 9.4% S-corp/pass-through tax

evidence.verbatim_quote: Convenes July 27, 2026 ... House vote: deadlocked 19-19 ... Senate vote: passed 11-8 ... a pass-through entities tax (S-corp tax) at 9.4%

verified_claims.notes: BACKGROUND / energy backdrop only; do not assert an AI link.

dropped.reason: Cook Inlet gas share 30% to under 1% not on any fetched page.
SB 250 death unverified; ADN names HB 259 (Rep. Mears) instead. Replaced by C5.
60 road miles north of Anchorage not stated in fetched sources.
$16/Mcf in-state gas not in the fetched article.
